data.content:
### Select Model in OpenCode

Source: https://docs.z.ai/scenario-example/develop-tools/opencode

Demonstrates how to change the AI model used by OpenCode. By typing the '/models' command, users can view available models and select a specific one, such as GLM-4.7.

```bash
/models
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Model API with Python

Source: https://docs.z.ai/guides/vlm/autoglm-phone-multilingual

This snippet shows how to configure and run the Model API using a Python script. It requires specifying the base URL for the inference API, the model name, and an API key. The script is executed from the command line.

```python
python main.py --base-url https://api-inference.modelscope.cn/v1 --model "ZAI/AutoGLM-Phone-9B" --apikey "your-zai-api-key" ""Open Chrome browser"
```

--------------------------------

### Configure GLM Models in Claude Code

Source: https://docs.z.ai/devpack/faq

This snippet demonstrates how to configure environment variables to map Claude Code's default models to Z.ai's GLM models. It specifies the default mappings for Opus, Sonnet, and Haiku, and explains how to modify the configuration file (~/.claude/settings.json) for custom model selections.

```json
{
  "ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL": "GLM-4.7",
  "ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL": "GLM-4.7",
  "ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL": "GLM-4.5-Air"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Select Z.AI Model in Factory Droid

Source: https://docs.z.ai/devpack/tool/droid

Command used within the running Factory Droid CLI to select the desired Z.AI GLM model. Custom models configured via BYOK will appear in a separate section.

```bash
/model
```

--------------------------------

### Update Model Identifier to GLM-4.7

Source: https://docs.z.ai/guides/overview/migrate-to-glm-new

Update the 'model' parameter in your API call to 'glm-4.7' to utilize the latest coding model. This is the first step in the migration process.

```python
resp = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="glm-4.7",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Briefly describe the advantages of GLM-4.7"}]
)
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Factory Droid with Z.AI GLM (OpenAI Protocol)

Source: https://docs.z.ai/devpack/tool/droid

Configures Factory Droid to use Z.AI's GLM models via the OpenAI compatible API. This JSON configuration specifies the model name, base URL, API key, and provider details for custom model integration.

```json
{
  "custom_models": [
    {
      "model_display_name": "GLM-4.7 [Z.AI Coding Plan]",
      "model": "glm-4.7",
      "base_url": "https://api.z.ai/api/coding/paas/v4",
      "api_key": "your_api_key",
      "provider": "generic-chat-completion-api",
      "max_tokens": 131072
    }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Update Model Identifier

Source: https://docs.z.ai/guides/overview/migrate-to-glm-new

Update the model identifier in your API call to 'glm-4.7' to utilize the latest GLM model.

```APIDOC
## POST /v1/chat/completions

### Description
Submits a chat completion request to the GLM model.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v1/chat/completions

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **model** (string) - Required - The identifier of the model to use for completion. Update to `glm-4.7`.
- **messages** (array) - Required - A list of message objects representing the conversation history.

### Request Example
```python
resp = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="glm-4.7",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Briefly describe the advantages of GLM-4.7"}]
)
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **choices** (array) - An array of completion choices.
- **choices[].message** (object) - The generated message.
- **choices[].message.content** (string) - The content of the message.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "id": "chatcmpl-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx",
  "object": "chat.completion",
  "created": 1677652288,
  "model": "glm-4.7",
  "choices": [
    {
      "index": 0,
      "message": {
        "role": "assistant",
        "content": "GLM-4.7 offers significant improvements including larger context and output support (up to 200K context and 128K output), real-time streaming of tool calls, enhanced deep thinking capabilities for complex reasoning, and superior code performance."
      },
      "finish_reason": "stop"
    }
  ],
  "usage": {
    "prompt_tokens": 20,
    "completion_tokens": 50,
    "total_tokens": 70
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Launch Grok CLI with a Model

Source: https://docs.z.ai/scenario-example/develop-tools/gork

Launches the Grok CLI, specifying the GLM model to use for interactions. Replace 'glm-4.7' with your desired model identifier.

```bash
grok --model glm-4.7
```

--------------------------------

### POST /api/paas/v4/images/generations

Source: https://docs.z.ai/guides/image/cogview-4

Generates an image based on the provided prompt and model. Requires authentication.

```APIDOC
## POST /api/paas/v4/images/generations

### Description
Generates an image based on the provided prompt and model. Requires authentication.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/api/paas/v4/images/generations

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
- **model** (string) - Required - The model to use for image generation (e.g., "cogView-4-250304").
- **prompt** (string) - Required - The text description of the image to generate.
- **size** (string) - Optional - The desired size of the generated image (e.g., "1024x1024").

### Request Example
```json
{
    "model": "cogView-4-250304",
    "prompt": "A cute little kitten sitting on a sunny windowsill, with the background of blue sky and white clouds.",
    "size": "1024x1024"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **data** (array) - Contains information about the generated image(s).
  - **url** (string) - The URL of the generated image.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "url": "https://example.com/image.png"
    }
  ]
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Claude Code Model Mappings

Source: https://docs.z.ai/devpack/tool/claude

This snippet shows how to manually configure environment variables in `settings.json` to map Claude Code's internal model names to specific GLM models. Ensure correct JSON formatting and restart Claude Code for changes to apply.

```json
{
  "env": {
    "ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL": "glm-4.5-air",
    "ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL": "glm-4.7",
    "ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL": "glm-4.7"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Function Object

Source: https://docs.z.ai/api-reference/llm/chat-completion

Defines a function that can be called by the model, including its name, description, and parameters.

```APIDOC
## Function Object

### Description
Defines a function that can be called by the model, including its name, description, and parameters.

### Properties
- **name** (string) - Description: The name of the function to be called. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64. minLength: 1, maxLength: 64, pattern: `^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$`.
- **description** (string) - Description: A description of what the function does, used by the model to choose when and how to call the function.
- **parameters** (object) - Schema for the function's parameters. Reference: `#/components/schemas/FunctionParameters`.
```

--------------------------------

### POST /paas/v4/videos/generations

Source: https://docs.z.ai/api-reference/video/generate-video

Generates videos using CogVideoX or Vidu models. Supports text-to-video, image-to-video, and frame-to-video generation. You can specify various parameters like model, prompt, quality, audio, size, and FPS.

```APIDOC
## POST /paas/v4/videos/generations

### Description
This endpoint allows for video generation using Z.AI's powerful models like CogVideoX and Vidu. You can create videos from text descriptions, images, or sequences of frames. The API supports various customization options to tailor the video output.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/paas/v4/videos/generations

### Parameters
#### Header Parameters
- **Accept-Language** (string) - Optional - Configures the desired response language for HTTP requests. Defaults to 'en-US,en'.

#### Request Body
(One of the following schemas)

**CogVideoX3Request**:
- **model** (string) - Required - The model code to be called. Must be 'cogvideox-3'.
- **prompt** (string) - Optional - Text description of the video, maximum input length of 512 characters. Either `image_url` or `prompt` must be provided, or both.
- **quality** (string) - Optional - Output mode. Can be 'speed' (prioritizes speed) or 'quality' (prioritizes quality). Defaults to 'speed'.
- **with_audio** (boolean) - Optional - Whether to generate AI sound effects. Defaults to `false`.
- **image_url** (array) - Optional - URLs of images to be used for video generation.
- **size** (string) - Optional - Desired video resolution, e.g., '1920x1080'.
- **fps** (integer) - Optional - Frames per second for the generated video.

**ViduText2VideoRequest**:
- **model** (string) - Required - The model code to be called. Must be 'vidu'.
- **prompt** (string) - Required - Text description of the video.
- **quality** (string) - Optional - Output mode. Can be 'speed' or 'quality'.
- **with_audio** (boolean) - Optional - Whether to generate AI sound effects. Defaults to `false`.
- **size** (string) - Optional - Desired video resolution, e.g., '1920x1080'.
- **fps** (integer) - Optional - Frames per second for the generated video.

**ViduImage2VideoRequest**:
- **model** (string) - Required - The model code to be called. Must be 'vidu'.
- **image_url** (string) - Required - URL of the input image.
- **prompt** (string) - Optional - Text description to guide video generation.
- **quality** (string) - Optional - Output mode. Can be 'speed' or 'quality'.
- **with_audio** (boolean) - Optional - Whether to generate AI sound effects. Defaults to `false`.
- **size** (string) - Optional - Desired video resolution, e.g., '1920x1080'.
- **fps** (integer) - Optional - Frames per second for the generated video.

**ViduFrames2VideoRequest**:
- **model** (string) - Required - The model code to be called. Must be 'vidu'.
- **image_url** (array) - Required - URLs of the first and last frames for video generation.
- **prompt** (string) - Optional - Text description to guide video generation.
- **quality** (string) - Optional - Output mode. Can be 'speed' or 'quality'.
- **with_audio** (boolean) - Optional - Whether to generate AI sound effects. Defaults to `false`.
- **size** (string) - Optional - Desired video resolution, e.g., '1920x1080'.
- **fps** (integer) - Optional - Frames per second for the generated video.

**ViduReference2VideoRequest**:
- **model** (string) - Required - The model code to be called. Must be 'vidu'.
- **prompt** (string) - Required - Text description of the video.
- **quality** (string) - Optional - Output mode. Can be 'speed' or 'quality'.
- **with_audio** (boolean) - Optional - Whether to generate AI sound effects. Defaults to `false`.
- **size** (string) - Optional - Desired video resolution, e.g., '1920x1080'.
- **fps** (integer) - Optional - Frames per second for the generated video.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "model": "cogvideox-3",
  "prompt": "A cat is playing with a ball.",
  "quality": "quality",
  "with_audio": true,
  "size": "1920x1080",
  "fps": 30
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **id** (string) - Unique identifier for the video generation task.
- **status** (string) - Current status of the video generation task (e.g., 'processing', 'completed', 'failed').
- **created_at** (string) - Timestamp when the task was created.
- **updated_at** (string) - Timestamp when the task was last updated.
- **video_url** (string) - URL of the generated video (if status is 'completed').

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "id": "gen_12345abcde",
  "status": "processing",
  "created_at": "2023-10-27T10:00:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2023-10-27T10:05:00Z",
  "video_url": null
}
```

#### Error Response (Default)
- **code** (integer) - Error code.
- **message** (string) - Detailed error message.

#### Error Response Example
```json
{
  "code": 400,
  "message": "Invalid input parameters."
}
```
```
